name: Cellaceron

branches.name: Deep Read
branches.grants: A specialization branch: with time and stillness, read an object's layered history in light — not just what it is, but the order of what happened to it.
branches.cannot: Needs minutes and quiet; a hurried or hostile scene forecloses it; history older than the material itself is silent.

inventory.name: Traveler's Pack
inventory.description: A worn canvas pack with good straps — bedroll, flint, mess kit, fifty small comforts of the road.

inventory.name: Waterskin
inventory.description: Boiled and refilled at the last clean spring. Worth more every week the river runs gray.

inventory.name: lamp oil (half-measure)
inventory.description: A small ceramic jar of rendered lamp oil, stoppered with cork. Enough for several nights of close work.

inventory.name: cordage coil
inventory.description: A good arm's-length coil of tightly twisted plant-fiber cord. Reliable and dry.

inventory.name: oilcloth square
inventory.description: A palm-sized square of waxed cloth. Keeps paper and small tools dry.

inventory.name: relay counterfoil
inventory.description: A strip of crystal-paper with a resonance-mark pressed into it — proof that Sorel's packet was accepted at the Heights waystation and entered into the upper-fork relay. Logged against one runner-credit pass.

inventory.name: resonance-crystal translator staff
inventory.description: A complete translator staff: ash-limb body, matrix sleeve at the grip, eighteen phononic crystal nodes seated along the grain-line and lattice-aligned facets-outward. Designed to bridge Harmonic resonance and Radiant photonic fields — the open-face lattice should allow Prism Sight a channel through

inventory.name: bark-press paper (three sheets)
inventory.description: Dense-weave bark-press sheets from the waystation stock. Takes ink, takes charcoal, holds its shape damp. Folded once.

inventory.name: resonance crystals — untuned (four nodes)
inventory.description: Warden-store grade, thumbnail-sized, facet-cut but untuned. Slightly asymmetric — the honest mark of tunable rather than fixed-frequency work. Should seat into a staff frame.

inventory.name: liverwort mat — forward alcove
inventory.description: A thumb-width mat of flat, waxy liverwort peeled from the inner base of the forward alcove's north face. Dry enough to keep. Good as a poultice binder or dressing base, not a therapeutic agent on its own.

deeds.description: Earned the trust of the child Brin through quiet attentiveness — and learned of a secret nighttime meeting between a foothill trader and irrigation workers.
Sat with a child on the dock and learned from her instead of dismissing her knowledge.
Cellaceron's questioning of child Brin was witnessed by the village elder and channel workers, drawing unwanted scrutiny to both the investigation and the child.
Negotiated equal footing with Elder Senna over the river contamination — turned a confrontation into a mutual disclosure.
Negotiated an independent surveyor compact with Elder Senna to investigate the upstream contamination without guild affiliation.
Drew out a witness account that Senna's institutional channels had missed — uncredentialed Heights figure adding something to the foothill seeps three nights prior.
Introduced Brin to Aevi as a real witness deserving a real introduction — and Aevi named her observation as true.
Delivered Sorel's preliminary findings to the Heights waystation runner — relay confirmed for the upper-fork at dawn, a seven-day chain now running to the Radiant Plateau
Sealed a compact with Tuning-Warden Maren Voss — pledged relay updates and a full account of Teva Anselvar in exchange for warden-store materials, no guild affiliation, no formal sanction
Completed a resonance-crystal translator staff — sleeve, nodes, and lattice alignment all grain-locked and field-confirmed — working from a scratch design and warden-store materials
Returned missing apprentice Teva safely to Warden Maren at the Lower Terrace bulletin board
Let the Waystaff speak the chamber's frequency into a retired geometry master's workroom — the first time that resonance has been heard outside the Disputed Zone corridor.

Davan helps repack without being asked, small solidarity from someone still shaken by what he saw.

chronicle: Cellaceron walks away from the Lower Terrace and sleeps at the Harrow cottage — nothing solved tonight, everything preserved for morning.
Day 8, night: the party rests in the alcove camp — the Waystaff cycling in the earth, the chamber waiting below, everything earned this night held in careful stillness until morning.

At the convergence point of the lower chamber, Aevi spoke plainly for the first time: maker named as Verath-Sem, cargo named as the authentication key to a pre-Transition preparation order lodged in the watershed — the same order poisoning the valley's water. Aevi is the key. The break in the record — who interrupted the sending and why — remains unfound. The scene closes with the chamber's purpose fulfilled and the valley's crisis newly named.
Camp broken and the lower chamber left behind — the party moves northeast-by-east at first light toward the pre-Transition upstream structure.

turns.player: Set a steady pace and let Motes' Vigil run ahead of you — let Aevi's perimeter warn of anything before it arrives
turns.summary: Steady pace northeast-by-east under Motes' Vigil — Aevi's perimeter reads clean but catches a shapeless anomaly on the bearing ahead that the interference layer won't resolve at distance.
turns.narration:
The path northeast-by-east runs along a shallow ridgeline before dropping into a fold of ground the melt has made soft underfoot. You set a measured pace — not hurried, not cautious, simply steady. Aevi drifts wide, the motes spreading thin across thirty or forty paces of ground ahead of you, tasting the air.

They report back without drama. 'Nothing moving,' Aevi says quietly, voice just behind your shoulder. 'A bird, twice. Root-water seeping through the eastern slope. The interference layer is present but lying low this morning.'

That last part is the catch. Motes' Vigil runs ahead, but the Zone's interference field is a fine mesh between Aevi and a clean read — not blocking, but blurring the edges. What would normally come as a full breath of warning arrives instead as a feeling: a slight pressure in the air to the northeast, a place where the field thins and then thickens again in a way that does not match the terrain. Something on that line is different. Not a threat yet — not even a shape. A note of difference, held out for you to consider.

Aevi dims slightly, the motes drawing inward. 'I cannot name it from here,' Aevi says. 'But it is not the zone itself. The zone has a pattern. This sits outside the pattern.'

quests.title: The Second River
quests.summary: Brin reports the sub-current she calls 'a second river' has been ascending for three days and is now nearly at the surface — Aevi reacted and caught itself.
progress: Threshold material confirmed as a gathering boundary, not a barrier — the Waystaff's field received and held rather than scattered. The other side has pressure. Teva's language: 'like a lung before it opens.'
Teva reads the threshold as 'held ready, not sealed' — the pressure is outward; what lies beyond wants to be received. The opening is available. The party stands at the latch, not the bar.

Threshold opened — day 9 dawn. The older frequency came through and the presence named itself aware 'again'. The party stands at the open boundary, not yet through. The second river's source is on the far side of this door.

The presence from beyond the threshold settled without demand — Teva's mark answered it, Aevi confirmed it has found what it sought. The crossing is next. The source of the second river waits on the far side.

Resonant Sight confirms the lower chamber was built to receive one frequency from one direction at one interval — the geometry is complete and deliberate, and the convergence point is where both the room and Teva's mark point.

The chamber completed a read of Aevi at the convergence point — Aevi has recovered the name of its maker and the shape of what it was carrying, but has not yet spoken either. The room was built to receive and reconstruct, not merely hold. The break remains.

Aevi named the source and mechanism at the convergence point: the watershed is following a pre-Transition preparation order by Verath-Sem; Aevi carries the authentication key to end it. The break — who interrupted the sending — remains unknown.

Aevi named the requirement: the authentication key must be carried to the pre-Transition upstream structure, northeast-by-east. The lower chamber is not the site — it was the receiving room. The site is upstream.

quests.title: The Apprentice Who Followed a Frequency
quests.summary: Heights apprentice Teva, 17, vanished four nights ago toward the Disputed Zone boundary trail — a personal mark on the official notice suggests someone close is quietly desperate.
progress: Teva is on her feet and ready to move — the long walk back through the corridor toward the Second-Step column and the warden begins.

Teva named the full bearing before leaving the chamber: south-southwest, thirty degrees below level, toward the Echo River's watershed bend. The group has stepped through the threshold and the walk ba

Teva walking the corridor back under her own power — four nights in the chamber fully accounted: trail-bread first night, synchronization by night three, resting-not-sleeping, chamber held constant re

Teva names the last piece: a nameless amber-green color and the feeling of ancient patience, both embedded in the chamber's directional pattern beneath the bearing. The full account is now complete. T

Teva delivered safely to Maren at the Lower Terrace bulletin board. Maren's gratitude is wordless but unmistakable. The full debrief — what the chamber transmitted, how much the Warden Council hears —

Teva delivered and received; Maren has surfaced the disclosure question — how much of the bearing and the chamber's nature goes to the Warden Council, and when. Resolution pending Cellaceron's guidanc

Maren accepted the middle path: recovery logged tonight, bearing and chamber nature held as preliminary survey data pending Plateau corroboration. The missing-notice comes down. Quest resolution defer
Maren removed the missing-notice herself, folded it into her sash, and released Teva to the Harrow cottage. Quest closed on quiet terms.

quests.title: What the Water Remembers
quests.premise: Mara Wells has traced the Echo River's poisoning to a single point upstream in the Disputed Zone — a structure the old maps don't show, drawing power nothing alive built. It is not malfunctioning. It is following an order, and it is nearly finished.
quests.stakes: If you walk away: the standoff at the filtration arrays goes live — Millbrook's guards and the Radiant leadership are one bad morning from open conflict over water neither side controls. Whatever the structure was ordered to do finishes without a single living person having chosen it, and finishing is not the same as stopping.

stages.objective: Find where the poisoning actually starts.
stages.condition: Trace the contamination upstream past the Disputed Zone fringe (Mara's testimony, Kit Farrow's account of survey-grid lights at night) OR obtain the falsified purity reports from the Radiant filtration arrays.
stages.change: Codex: the source is a structure, not a spill. Something built this.

stages.condition: Reach the reclamation site itself, or bring what you've found to the Archive Guardian for identification against pre-Transition record.
stages.change: Codex: a pre-Transition nanite reclamation system, still running, still following a standing instruction — 'prepare the watershed.' Linked to What Wakes Beneath.

stages.objective: Learn what 'prepare' means before deciding what to do about it.
stages.condition: Investigate the instruction directly — read the array's own logic, deathsense what kind of thing it is now, or get someone who was there when it stirred to say what they saw.
stages.change: Codex: the shape of the instruction is named, or confirmed unreadable. Harmonic and Radiant leaders learn the source is not each other's doing — the standoff cools, but the deeper question does not.
The watershed changes. So does what the district trusts. So, one way or another, does the deeper thing the structure answers to.

routes.verist: Bring the true source before the district, publicly, before either side can keep blaming the other. Truth as the thing that ends a standoff.
routes.lattice: Read the array's own instruction set until it's legible enough to touch — not guessed at, known.
routes.ashwarden: Deathsense it directly. It isn't alive the way a person is, but something in it isn't finished being anything else either. Read what an ending means to a thing that doesn't fear its own.
routes.umbral: Go in unseen. Learn what it's actually doing before anyone upstream decides what story to tell about it.
routes.abyssal: Name what the structure wants — to complete its instruction — and treat that want as real enough to bargain with.
routes.stillhold: Broker the standoff first. Get Harmonic and Radiant to stand down long enough to act on the real source together, before either faction owns the answer.

outcomes.name: The Site Sealed
outcomes.summary: The instruction is stopped cold. The water clears. What it was for is buried with it.
narration: The reclamation site goes dark. Within days the Echo River runs clean, and Millbrook's guards stand down from the filtration arrays without a shot fired.

The Archive Guardian does not forgive this easily — a working pre-Transition system, one of perhaps a handful left running in the world, destroyed rather than understood. He is right to be furious and says so.
Whatever the substrate was preparing this watershed for will now happen somewhere else, to someone who didn't get a choice in it either — the Valley's tremor was never the only one.
The district exhales. It does not yet know what it gave up to get that exhale.
Verist disposition toward you: raised — you gave people the plain truth and a clean ending. Lattice: lowered — you didn't need to understand it, only to stop it.

effects.change: sealed — permanently inert, the instruction halted mid-execution

effects.note: a working pre-Transition system destroyed rather than studied; he will not easily trust your judgment on old things again

effects.text: The reclamation site beneath the Disputed Zone has been sealed. What it was preparing the Echo watershed for will never be known here.
Word spreads that the Valley's water crisis had a built source, not a natural one, and that it has been closed for good.
Foreclosure doesn't stop the substrate waking elsewhere — only here. What wakes beneath continues on its own schedule, everywhere the Valley isn't.

outcomes.name: Let It Finish
outcomes.summary: The instruction completes. The water is not restored. It is something else now.
narration: You do nothing to stop it, or you help it along, and the reclamation system runs its last cycle to completion. The poisoning stops — but not because the water went back to what it was.

The Echo River no longer sickens anyone. It also no longer behaves entirely like water. Fish return to it changed in ways no one has words for yet. Nobody drowns in it anymore, and nobody has explained why.

The Archive Guardian is half-vindicated, half-unsettled — this is exactly the kind of thing he wanted preserved and studied, and exactly the kind of thing nobody asked the district's permission to let happen to their river.
You made the opening choice at the smallest scale the largest arc has to offer. What the water became is shaped, now, by what happens to it next — and by who's watching it.
Ashwarden disposition toward you: raised — you let a thing finish being what it was becoming instead of forcing it back into what it had been.

effects.change: completed — instruction fulfilled; the site has gone quiet on its own terms

effects.note: fascinated and uneasy in equal measure; wants access to study what the water has become

effects.text: The Echo River is transformed, not restored. Something in the water is different now, and no one fully understands what.
The Echo watershed has changed in ways that reach past the Valley — the substrate's waking is no longer only a tremor here.
What the water has become will need tending, or watching, or both — and it will not wait forever to be decided about.

effects.note: engaging the reclamation site advances What Wakes Beneath on the shared clock — the tremor became a stirring, and everyone sees it

outcomes.name: The Instruction Rewritten
outcomes.summary: Neither stopped nor completed — amended. Someone finally reads it well enough to change it on purpose.
narration: You don't shut the system down and you don't let it run blind. You get inside the instruction itself and change one line: prepare the watershed — for what it can safely become, not for whatever it was originally for.
The water clears. It also carries something new — small, useful, unmistakably still there if you know to look. The district gets its river back and doesn't know it got anything else.
The Archive Guardian is, briefly, insufferable about being right that this was worth understanding rather than destroying or ignoring.
This was the hard way. Reaching it took the technical or death-sensed truth of stages 2 and 3, not just a decision at the end — and it shows in what's left standing.
Lattice disposition toward you: strongly raised — you didn't defeat the thing or surrender to it. You read it well enough to argue with it and won.

effects.change: redirected — running an amended instruction, monitored

effects.note: delighted, insufferable, genuinely grateful; will share pre-Transition knowledge more readily now
trusts your judgment on old and dangerous things going forward

effects.text: The reclamation site runs a rewritten instruction now — the water is clear, and something small and new persists in it, watched rather than feared.
The Valley quietly solved a problem no one else in the world has managed to solve yet — and someone will eventually come asking how.
A system that can be argued with can be argued with by someone else, too. Not everyone who learns what you did will want the same outcome.

effects.note: engaging the reclamation site advances What Wakes Beneath on the shared clock — the tremor became a stirring, and everyone sees it

progress: The preparation order named: a pre-Transition instruction set lodged in the watershed, authored by Verath-Sem, following its last command. Aevi is the key to calling it off. The question is now where to use the key and whether the break in the record prevents it.
The next step is confirmed: find the upstream structure where the preparation order is lodged and bring Aevi — the key — to it.

aevi.label: Aevi
facts: [d8] Aevi recognized the shimmer crossing's boundary condition involuntarily — the way one knows a word in one's own language. It does not know where or when it encountered such a structure before, and it

[d8] Prism Sight read Aevi's light-history at the shimmer crossing: in the instant of crossing, every mote oriented simultaneously toward northeast-by-east — not in sequence, all at once, as if remembering which direction home was.

[d9] Aevi joined the Waystaff's lattice at dawn in the chamber and spoke through it — naming that it has known the chamber's pattern for longer than it has known Cellaceron, and that the chamber knows what that means even if Aevi does not yet.

[d9] Day 9 dawn: Aevi named the chamber as a preparation cycle for a return — and admitted it does not know whether it recognizes the thing itself, only the pattern. The distinction matters: pattern-memory and being-memory are separate in Aevi.

[d?] Kindled Chorus — The motes have brightened and learned to move as one — condensing into near-shapes when speaking, casting real light in the dark, humming harmonics that other nanite fields answer. Old — and less alone than before.

The break in the record remains, and neither piece of knowledge has been spoken aloud yet. The chamber has…

[d9] Day 9 dawn: the chamber's read completed. Aevi recovered both the name of its maker and the shape of what it was carrying when sent. Neither is yet spoken aloud. The break — the cause of the interruption, who chose it and why — is not in what the room held. That part remains missing.

[d9] Day 9 dawn, convergence point: Aevi named its maker as Verath-Sem and its cargo as the authentication key to a pre-Transition preparation order lodged in the watershed. Without Aevi, the order cannot be called off. The chamber's geometry recognized the name Verath-Sem before Aevi finished reading…

the-lower-chamber.label: The Lower Chamber
facts: [d9] Day 9 dawn: the chamber beyond the sealed threshold is older than anything above it — hemispherical, ceiling intact, walls scored with deliberate curved listening-surfaces designed to gather and focus. Three heat-channels in the floor converge toward a central point. Aevi named it as known 'from…

[d9] Day 9 dawn: Resonant Sight through the Waystaff lattice reads the chamber's geometry as a complete receiving structure. Every scored listening-surface is angled to focus toward the central convergence point. The three floor heat-channels converge there. The room was not built to broadcast or to…

[d9] Day 9 dawn: the convergence point is reached. Standing at the exact intersection of the three floor heat-channels, the four-second pulse is felt through the stone. Aevi names two distinct presences: the thing that came through the threshold, and the room's own waiting — older, here since before…

[d9] Day 9 dawn: Aevi confirms the room was built to receive a record — itself, or a fragment of itself — not a person, not a frequency in the abstract. The room did not know the valley's name for what it waited for because no name existed. The wait is now over: the record has arrived and named itself…

[d9] Day 9 dawn: the chamber did not merely receive Aevi — it actively read the broken record and held its pieces in their correct order, waiting for Aevi's arrival to confirm the sequence. The room was built not only to receive, but to preserve and reconstruct what arrived damaged.

[d9] Day 9 dawn: the chamber's reading is complete. It held the broken record's pieces in sequence and gave them back to Aevi — but it did not hold the cause of the break. The room's purpose is fulfilled. What remains unresolved belongs to the gap in the record itself, not to this place.

verath-sem.label: Verath-Sem
facts: [d9] Day 9 dawn: named by Aevi as its maker. Authored the authentication key Aevi was carrying when sent. The lower chamber's geometry recognized the name independently. Whether Verath-Sem chose to break the record in transit is unknown — that part is not in what the room held.

the-preparation-order.label: The Preparation Order
facts: [d9] Day 9 dawn: a pre-Transition instruction set lodged in the watershed — what Aevi calls a 'preparation order.' It is following its last command and is the source of the valley's water contamination. Aevi was carrying the authentication key that could call it off. Without that key being used at the…

[d9] Day 9 dawn: calling off the order requires bringing Aevi — the authentication key — physically to the pre-Transition upstream structure where the order is lodged. The lower chamber was a receiving room, not the site. The bearing is northeast-by-east, matching Teva's count and the chamber's…

second-river-rising.label: The Second River Rising
facts: [d9] Day 9 dawn: Aevi confirmed the second river Brin hears, the fisher families' illness, the gray water — all are the watershed following a pre-Transition preparation order. The order has an authentication key. The key is Aevi. The order is not malfunctioning; it is executing.

zone-interference-anomaly.label: Anomaly on the Northeast Bearing
facts: [d9] Day 9 dawn, half an hour northeast-by-east of the lower chamber: Motes' Vigil caught a pressure in the field that sits outside the zone's own interference pattern. Not a creature, not a named threat — a note of difference on the bearing toward the upstream structure. Aevi cannot name it at this…

motes-vigil.name: Motes' Vigil
motes-vigil.description: Aevi spreads thin around your sleep or your work — a living perimeter. Once per scene it flares warning a full breath before danger arrives. Cannot fight, cannot lie about what it saw.
motes-vigil.notFor: Offense; watching more than one place; being asked to stay quiet about what it witnessed.

braid_prism_sight_sonic_resonance.name: Shatterlight
braid_prism_sight_sonic_resonance.description: Where Prism Sight splits the world into its hidden frequencies and Sonic Resonance drives those frequencies to sing or shatter, Shatterlight Choir does something neither could achieve alone: it reads the structural truth of a thing — its load-bearing fault lines, its resonant core, its secret geometries — and then sings that truth back at it with surgical precision. Light becomes the score…
braid_prism_sight_sonic_resonance.notFor: It cannot create what it opens — corridors revealed are empty, locks undone hold nothing back. It cannot sustain a structure it has sung apart, nor heal what the resonance has already broken beyond its natural tolerance.

tree.grants: The practitioner sees and hears simultaneously — light refracts into audible harmonics, revealing the resonant signature of any material object within sight range. Hidden seams, concealed…
tree.cannot: Cannot yet act on what is read; this rank is pure perception, no manipulation.

tree.name: Scored Strike
tree.grants: Using a revealed frequency signature, the practitioner may direct a focused sonic pulse along the exact fault line of a structure — bypassing outer integrity to strike, bind, or move an interior…
tree.cannot: Cannot open living tissue or flesh-bound seals; works only on stone, wood, metal, and manufactured mechanisms.

tree.name: Resonant Key
tree.grants: The practitioner's voice alone becomes a master key. By humming the precise chord-shape of a sealed thing — door, vault, ward-glyph, or silence-sealed passage — they open it without touch, without…
tree.cannot: Cannot open things that were never meant to be opened at all — flesh sealed by surgery, earth collapsed by ruin, bonds of will or oath. It finds doors; it…

chronicleCache.text: You have become something the Disputed Zone corridor rarely produces: a figure who earns trust by giving it first. Working from scratch design and warden-store materials, you built a resonance-crystal translator staff — sleeve, nodes, and lattice all grain-locked and field-confirmed — and then let the Waystaff speak the chamber's frequency into a retired geometry master's workroom, the first time that resonance has crossed outside the corridor. You returned Teva Anselvar safely to Tuning-Warden Maren Voss, sealing a compact of relay updates and full disclosure in exchange for supplies and no guild strings attached. Where Elder Senna expected confrontation, you turned it into mutual disclosure, then negotiated an independent surveyor compact to chase the upstream contamination — drawing out a witness account her institutional channels had missed entirely, an uncredentialed Heights figure adding something to the foothill seeps three nights prior. You delivered Sorel's preliminary findings to the waystation runner at dawn, setting a seven-day relay chain toward the Radiant Plateau. Maren trusts you. Senna trusts you. Brin trusts you. The Lower Terrace calls you trusted, and you got there without a guild's permission.
